revision.text: #redirect[[en:Oni-no-Iwaya_Kofun_(Iki_City)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
#redirect[[en:Oni no Iwaya Kofun (Iki)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼の窟古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), entrance.jpg|250px]]
石室開口部 |所属 = |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触1206-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|48|4.57|N|129|42|45.42|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline|name=鬼の窟古墳}} |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径45m
高さ13m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（3室）
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]か） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末頃 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 須恵器・新羅系陶質土器 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = }} '''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、壱岐市芦辺町国分本村触にある古墳。形状は円墳。壱岐島を代表する巨石墳である{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 本古墳は[[1936年]]（昭和11年）に京都大学による石室の実測調査が、1970年（昭和45年）に九州大学による石室の実測調査が、[[1989年]]（平成元年）に旧芦辺町教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされている{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は円形で、直径45メートル・高さ13メートルを測る{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は玄武岩の巨石で作られた複室構造の両袖式横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成される{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 :* 石室全長：16.5メートル - 長崎県内で最長[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/224 鬼の窟古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 :* 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅3メートル、高さ3.3メートル :* 中室：長さ3メートル、幅2.5メートル、高さ1.9メートル :* 前室：長さ3メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ1.9メートル 玄室には板石が残存し、これは組合式箱式石棺の部材と推測されている。本古墳からの出土品としては、須恵器・新羅系陶質土器などが見つかっている{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 この鬼の窟古墳の築造時期は、6世紀末頃と推定される。 ファイル:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 ファイル:Oninoiwaya-kofun bodo.jpg|墓道 ファイル:Oninoiwaya-kofun sendo.jpg|墓道から見た羨道と玄室

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼の窟古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), entrance.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触1206-1 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 4.57 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 45.42 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径45m
高さ13m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]か） |出土品 = 須恵器・新羅系陶質土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼の窟古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、壱岐市芦辺町国分本村触にある古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1936年]]（昭和11年）に京都大学による石室の実測調査が、1970年（昭和45年）に九州大学による石室の実測調査が、[[1989年]]（平成元年）に旧芦辺町教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされている{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は円形で、直径45メートル・高さ13メートルを測る{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は玄武岩の巨石で作られた複室構造の両袖式横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成される{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 :* 石室全長：16.5メートル - 長崎県内で最長[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/224 鬼の窟古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 :* 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅3メートル、高さ3.3メートル :* 中室：長さ3メートル、幅2.5メートル、高さ1.9メートル :* 前室：長さ3メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ1.9メートル 玄室には板石が残存し、これは組合式箱式石棺の部材と推測されている。本古墳からの出土品としては、須恵器・新羅系陶質土器などが見つかっている{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、6世紀末頃と推定される。 Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 Oninoiwaya-kofun bodo.jpg|羨道 Oninoiwaya-kofun sendo.jpg|前室 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=長崎県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15685 |title=鬼の窟古墳 -環境整備事業報告書-|volume=長崎県芦辺町文化財調査報告書第4集|publisher=芦辺町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - 高さ13m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]か） |出土品 = 須恵器・新羅系陶質土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼の窟古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触にある古墳。 形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 == [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1936年]]（昭和11年）に京都大学による石室の実測調査が、1970年（昭和45年）に九州大学による石室の実測調査が、[[1989年]]（平成元年）に旧芦辺町教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされている{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘は円形で、直径45メートル・高さ13メートルを測る{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は玄武岩の巨石で作られた複室構造の両袖式横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成される{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。その規模は次の通り{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 :* 石室全長：16.5メートル - 長崎県内で最長[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/224 鬼の窟古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。 :* 玄室：長さ3メートル、幅3メートル、高さ3.3メートル :* 中室：長さ3メートル、幅2.5メートル、高さ1.9メートル :* 前室：長さ3メートル、幅2.3メートル、高さ1.9メートル 玄室には板石が残存し、これは組合式箱式石棺の部材と推測されている。本古墳からの出土品としては、須恵器・新羅系陶質土器などが見つかっている{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 築造時期は、6世紀末頃と推定される。 Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), sekishitsu.jpg|石室内部 Oninoiwaya-kofun bodo.jpg|羨道 Oninoiwaya-kofun sendo.jpg|前室 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=長崎県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15685 |title=鬼の窟古墳 -環境整備事業報告書-|volume=長崎県芦辺町文化財調査報告書第4集|publisher=芦辺町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:おにのいわやこふん いきし}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: 全体加筆修正、画像追加と更新。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼の窟古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触1206-1（字磐屋森） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 4.57 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 45.42 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径45m
高さ13.5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]か） |出土品 = 鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼の窟古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触にある古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[壱岐古墳群]]」6基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造時期 |- |[[対馬塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長63m||6c後半 |- |[[双六古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長91m||6c後半 |- |[[笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|笹塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径66m||6c後半-末 |- |[[兵瀬古墳]]||円墳||直径54m||6c後半-末 |- |[[掛木古墳]]||円墳||直径18-22.5m||6c後半-末 |- |'''鬼の窟古墳'''||円墳||直径45m||6c後半-末 |}
壱岐島中央部のなだらかな尾根状丘陵上（標高約100メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[江戸時代]]後期にはすでに石室が開口し、[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）・[[1970年]]（昭和45年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に石室実測調査が実施されているほか、[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径45メートル・高さ約13.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造の石室で、石室全長16.5メートルを測る大型石室であり、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。石室内の調査では、副葬品として鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器などが検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃（IIIB-IVA型式期）と推定され、[[7世紀]]代を通じた追葬が想定される{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 古墳域は[[2009年]]（平成21年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）完成の『甲子夜話』に墳形・石室の実測図の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}。 * [[文久]]元年（[[1861年]]）完成の『壱岐名勝図誌』に古墳のスケッチ・法量の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）、石室実測調査（[[京都大学]]、未報告）。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年）、発掘調査（東亞考古学会、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）11月24日、長崎県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、石室実測調査（[[九州大学]]、[[1980年]]の論考に石室実測図掲載）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、石室羨道の天井石の亀裂にあたり鉄柱・コンクリートによる補強。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）8月、[[昭和62年台風第12号|台風12号]]で羨道開口部右側壁が崩壊{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、石室修復および墳丘測量・石室清掃・石室実測調査（芦辺町教育委員会、[[1990年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）2月12日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * 石室全長：16.5メートル * 玄室：長さ3.2メートル、幅3メートル、高さ3.3メートル * 中室（第1前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ1.75メートル * 前室（第2前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2メートル、高さ1.9メートル * 羨道：長さ5.1メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 開口部：幅1.9メートル、高さ2.4メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される史跡説明板。。玄室の平面形は正方形に近い方形で、中室・前室の平面形は羽子板状の長方形である{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。中室・前室・羨道はほぼ同じ高さである。石室の床面には前面に敷石を施す。前室の前面の閉塞石は1953年（昭和28年）に復元されている{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 玄室内には長さ2.55メートル・幅0.75メートル・厚さ0.13メートルの長方形の板石が遺存しており、組合式箱式石棺の部材とみられる{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。現在は奥壁前面に不動像が安置される。 1953年（昭和28年）の石室内の調査では、須恵器・土師器・半島系土器（新羅土器）・鉄器類（鏃）が検出されている。そのほか、記録には「鉄製鏡板」・「鉄地鍍金の鍔金具らしきもの」が見えるが、現在は所在不明のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。また1989年（平成元年）の調査では、須恵器・新羅土器が検出されている。 Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), chushitsu-2.jpg|中室（開口部方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), chushitsu-1.jpg|中室（玄室方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), zenshitsu-1.jpg|前室（玄室方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), entrance-2.jpg|開口部 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15685 |title=鬼の窟古墳 -環境整備事業報告書-|volume=長崎県芦辺町文化財調査報告書第4集|publisher=芦辺町教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=壱岐の島の古墳群～現状調査|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=壱岐古墳群|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 九州大学発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/90456|title=壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|volume=東亞考古学会壱岐原の辻遺跡調査報告書I|publisher=九州大学大学院人文科学研究院考古学研究室|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=長崎県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小田冨士雄|year=1980|chapter=対馬・壱岐の古墳文化|title=東アジアにおける日本古代史講座2|publisher=学生社|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼の窟古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触1206-1（字磐屋森） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 4.57 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 45.42 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径45m
高さ13.5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]か） |出土品 = 鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼の窟古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触にある古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[壱岐古墳群]]」6基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造時期 |- |[[対馬塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長63m||6c後半 |- |[[双六古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長91m||6c後半 |- |[[笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|笹塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径66m||6c後半-末 |- |[[兵瀬古墳]]||円墳||直径54m||6c後半-末 |- |[[掛木古墳]]||円墳||直径18-22.5m||6c後半-末 |- |'''鬼の窟古墳'''||円墳||直径45m||6c後半-末 |}
壱岐島中央部のなだらかな尾根状丘陵上（標高約100メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[江戸時代]]後期にはすでに石室が開口し、[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）・[[1970年]]（昭和45年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に石室実測調査が実施されているほか、[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径45メートル・高さ約13.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造で、石室全長16.5メートルを測る大型石室であり、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。石室内の調査では、副葬品として鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器などが検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃（IIIB-IVA型式期）と推定され、[[7世紀]]代を通じた追葬が想定される{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 古墳域は[[2009年]]（平成21年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）完成の『甲子夜話』に墳形・石室の実測図の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}。 * [[文久]]元年（[[1861年]]）完成の『壱岐名勝図誌』に古墳のスケッチ・法量の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）、石室実測調査（[[京都大学]]、未報告）。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年）、発掘調査（東亞考古学会、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）11月24日、長崎県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、石室実測調査（[[九州大学]]、[[1980年]]の論考に石室実測図掲載）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、石室羨道の天井石の亀裂にあたり鉄柱・コンクリートによる補強。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）8月、[[昭和62年台風第12号|台風12号]]で羨道開口部右側壁が崩壊{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、石室修復および墳丘測量・石室清掃・石室実測調査（芦辺町教育委員会、[[1990年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）2月12日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * 石室全長：16.5メートル * 玄室：長さ3.2メートル、幅3メートル、高さ3.3メートル * 中室（第1前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ1.75メートル * 前室（第2前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2メートル、高さ1.9メートル * 羨道：長さ5.1メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 開口部：幅1.9メートル、高さ2.4メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される史跡説明板。。玄室の平面形は正方形に近い方形で、中室・前室の平面形は羽子板状の長方形である{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。中室・前室・羨道はほぼ同じ高さである。石室の床面には前面に敷石を施す。前室の前面の閉塞石は1953年（昭和28年）に復元されている{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 玄室内には長さ2.55メートル・幅0.75メートル・厚さ0.13メートルの長方形の板石が遺存しており、組合式箱式石棺の部材とみられる{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。現在は奥壁前面に不動像が安置される。 1953年（昭和28年）の石室内の調査では、須恵器・土師器・半島系土器（新羅土器）・鉄器類（鏃）が検出されている。そのほか、記録には「鉄製鏡板」・「鉄地鍍金の鍔金具らしきもの」が見えるが、現在は所在不明のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。また1989年（平成元年）の調査では、須恵器・新羅土器が検出されている。 Oni-no-iwaya revision.text:
{{Infobox Ancient Burial Mound | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 48 | latitude_sec = 4.57 | longitude_deg = 129 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 45.42 | ISO = JP-42 | form = {{ill|Circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} | dimensions = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | burial_facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Circular burial mounds]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:original pages]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Circular burial mounds]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:original pages]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Circular burial mounds]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Circular burial mounds]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Circular burial mounds]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

Height 13.5 m | burial_facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 48 | latitude_sec = 4.57 | longitude_deg = 129 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 45.42 | ISO = JP-42 | form = {{ill|Circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} | dimensions = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | burial_facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 48 | latitude_sec = 4.57 | longitude_deg = 129 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 45.42 | ISO = JP-42 | form = {{ill|Circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} | dimensions = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | burial_facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 48 | latitude_sec = 4.57 | longitude_deg = 129 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 45.42 | ISO = JP-42 | form = {{ill|Circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} | dimensions = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | burial_facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - (possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Tumulus and entrance to the stone chamber | alias = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | latitude_deg = 33 | latitude_min = 48 | latitude_sec = 4.57 | longitude_deg = 129 | longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 45.42 | ISO = JP-42 | form = {{ill|Circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} | dimensions = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | burial_facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | artifacts = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | construction_date = Late 6th century | interred = | designation = Included in the National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | icon = Burial mound | label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | label_position = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular burial mound|ja|円墳}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}, the site was designated as part of the National Historic Site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12年}}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11年}}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28年}}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30年}}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36年}}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic Site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45年}}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55年}}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62年}}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21年}}: Included in the National Historic Site designation. == Burial Facility == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Tumulus Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Cultural Heritage Database] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]]
