By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|12=draft|12=draft}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

revision.comment: 7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市) up to rev 100827855
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼の窟古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触1206-1（字磐屋森） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 4.57 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 45.42 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径45m
高さ13.5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]か） |出土品 = 鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼の窟古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触にある古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[壱岐古墳群]]」6基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造時期 |- |[[対馬塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長63m||6c後半 |- |[[双六古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長91m||6c後半 |- |[[笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|笹塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径66m||6c後半-末 |- |[[兵瀬古墳]]||円墳||直径54m||6c後半-末 |- |[[掛木古墳]]||円墳||直径18-22.5m||6c後半-末 |- |'''鬼の窟古墳'''||円墳||直径45m||6c後半-末 |}
壱岐島中央部のなだらかな尾根状丘陵上（標高約100メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[江戸時代]]後期にはすでに石室が開口し、[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）・[[1970年]]（昭和45年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に石室実測調査が実施されているほか、[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径45メートル・高さ約13.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造で、石室全長16.5メートルを測る大型石室であり、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。石室内の調査では、副葬品として鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器などが検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃（IIIB-IVA型式期）と推定され、[[7世紀]]代を通じた追葬が想定される{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 古墳域は[[2009年]]（平成21年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）完成の『甲子夜話』に墳形・石室の実測図の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}。 * [[文久]]元年（[[1861年]]）完成の『壱岐名勝図誌』に古墳のスケッチ・法量の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）、石室実測調査（[[京都大学]]、未報告）。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年）、発掘調査（東亞考古学会、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）11月24日、長崎県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、石室実測調査（[[九州大学]]、[[1980年]]の論考に石室実測図掲載）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、石室羨道の天井石の亀裂にあたり鉄柱・コンクリートによる補強。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）8月、[[昭和62年台風第12号|台風12号]]で羨道開口部右側壁が崩壊{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、石室修復および墳丘測量・石室清掃・石室実測調査（芦辺町教育委員会、[[1990年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）2月12日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * 石室全長：16.5メートル * 玄室：長さ3.2メートル、幅3メートル、高さ3.3メートル * 中室（第1前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ1.75メートル * 前室（第2前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2メートル、高さ1.9メートル * 羨道：長さ5.1メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 開口部：幅1.9メートル、高さ2.4メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される史跡説明板。。玄室の平面形は正方形に近い方形で、中室・前室の平面形は羽子板状の長方形である{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。中室・前室・羨道はほぼ同じ高さである。石室の床面には前面に敷石を施す。前室の前面の閉塞石は1953年（昭和28年）に復元されている{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 玄室内には長さ2.55メートル・幅0.75メートル・厚さ0.13メートルの長方形の板石が遺存しており、組合式箱式石棺の部材とみられる{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。現在は奥壁前面に不動像が安置される。 1953年（昭和28年）の石室内の調査では、須恵器・土師器・半島系土器（新羅土器）・鉄器類（鏃）が検出されている。そのほか、記録には「鉄製鏡板」・「鉄地鍍金の鍔金具らしきもの」が見えるが、現在は所在不明のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。また1989年（平成元年）の調査では、須恵器・新羅土器が検出されている。 Oni-no-iwaya Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), zenshitsu-2.jpg|前室（開口部方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), zenshitsu-1.jpg|前室（玄室方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/90456|title=壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|series=東亞考古学会壱岐原の辻遺跡調査報告書I|publisher=九州大学大学院人文科学研究院考古学研究室|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=長崎県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] to [[Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鬼の窟古墳 |画像 = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触1206-1（字磐屋森） |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 4.57 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 45.42 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径45m
高さ13.5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]か） |出土品 = 鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鬼の窟古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鬼の窟古墳'''（おにのいわやこふん）は、[[壱岐市]]芦辺町国分本村触にある古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+史跡「[[壱岐古墳群]]」6基 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造時期 |- |[[対馬塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長63m||6c後半 |- |[[双六古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長91m||6c後半 |- |[[笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|笹塚古墳]]||[[円墳]]||直径66m||6c後半-末 |- |[[兵瀬古墳]]||円墳||直径54m||6c後半-末 |- |[[掛木古墳]]||円墳||直径18-22.5m||6c後半-末 |- |'''鬼の窟古墳'''||円墳||直径45m||6c後半-末 |}
壱岐島中央部のなだらかな尾根状丘陵上（標高約100メートル）に築造された古墳である。[[江戸時代]]後期にはすでに石室が開口し、[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）・[[1970年]]（昭和45年）・[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に石室実測調査が実施されているほか、[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径45メートル・高さ約13.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造で、石室全長16.5メートルを測る大型石室であり、石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の巨石が使用される。石室内の調査では、副葬品として鉄鏃・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・新羅土器などが検出されている。築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半-末頃（IIIB-IVA型式期）と推定され、[[7世紀]]代を通じた追葬が想定される{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 古墳域は[[2009年]]（平成21年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[天保]]12年（[[1841年]]）完成の『甲子夜話』に墳形・石室の実測図の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}。 * [[文久]]元年（[[1861年]]）完成の『壱岐名勝図誌』に古墳のスケッチ・法量の記載{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）、石室実測調査（[[京都大学]]、未報告）。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年）、発掘調査（東亞考古学会、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1961年]]（昭和36年）11月24日、長崎県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、石室実測調査（[[九州大学]]、[[1980年]]の論考に石室実測図掲載）{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、石室羨道の天井石の亀裂にあたり鉄柱・コンクリートによる補強。 * [[1987年]]（昭和62年）8月、[[昭和62年台風第12号|台風12号]]で羨道開口部右側壁が崩壊{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）、石室修復および墳丘測量・石室清掃・石室実測調査（芦辺町教育委員会、[[1990年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|鬼の窟古墳 環境整備事業報告書|1990}}。 * [[2009年]]（平成21年）2月12日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「壱岐古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。玄室・中室（第1前室）・前室（第2前室）・羨道からなる3室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 * 石室全長：16.5メートル * 玄室：長さ3.2メートル、幅3メートル、高さ3.3メートル * 中室（第1前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2.4メートル、高さ1.75メートル * 前室（第2前室）：長さ4.1メートル、幅2メートル、高さ1.9メートル * 羨道：長さ5.1メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 開口部：幅1.9メートル、高さ2.4メートル 石室の石材には[[玄武岩]]の自然石の巨石が使用される史跡説明板。。玄室の平面形は正方形に近い方形で、中室・前室の平面形は羽子板状の長方形である{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。中室・前室・羨道はほぼ同じ高さである。石室の床面には前面に敷石を施す。前室の前面の閉塞石は1953年（昭和28年）に復元されている{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。 玄室内には長さ2.55メートル・幅0.75メートル・厚さ0.13メートルの長方形の板石が遺存しており、組合式箱式石棺の部材とみられる{{Sfn|壱岐の島の古墳群|2012}}。現在は奥壁前面に不動像が安置される。 1953年（昭和28年）の石室内の調査では、須恵器・土師器・半島系土器（新羅土器）・鉄器類（鏃）が検出されている。そのほか、記録には「鉄製鏡板」・「鉄地鍍金の鍔金具らしきもの」が見えるが、現在は所在不明のため詳らかでない{{Sfn|壱岐原の辻閨繰遺跡・妙泉寺古墳群・鬼の窟古墳|2018}}。また1989年（平成元年）の調査では、須恵器・新羅土器が検出されている。 Oni-no-iwaya - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=鬼の窟古墳|title=長崎県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|鬼の窟古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]] {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=draft}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin||qq=draft}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. (possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki, Nagasaki|qq=}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic site|qq=}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]]

Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]] [[Category:壱岐古墳群]]

Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]] [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]]

The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Historic sites of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E9%AC%BC%E3%81%AE%E7%AA%9F%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3_(%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳]] [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]]

Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century.
