[[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]

It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}}

[[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki, Nagasaki|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

[[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | image = [[File:Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki), funkyu.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki, Nagasaki|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Ashibe Town, Kokubu Motomura Fure 1206-1 (Iwayamori District), Nagasaki Prefecture | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 4.57 | coordinates = 129 | coordinates = 42 | coordinates = 45.42 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter 45 m
Height 13.5 m | Burial Facility = Dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(possibly with a component stone {{ill|box-shaped sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}}) | burial goods = Iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, Silla pottery | associated_mounds = | built = Late 6th century | interred = | Historic site = Included in the National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | cultural_property = | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Kofun | map_dot_label = Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun''' (鬼の窟古墳) is a {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|en|Iki, Nagasaki|WD=Q861519|lt=Iki, Nagasaki|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, Ashibe Town, Nagasaki Prefecture. It is one of the tumuli comprising the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. == Overview == The tumulus is situated atop a gently sloping ridge in central Iki Island at an elevation of approximately 100 meters. By the late Edo period, the stone chamber had already been exposed, with detailed surveys conducted in {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}, {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}, and {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}. Excavations in {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }} also revealed notable features. The mound has a diameter of 45 meters and a height of approximately 13.5 meters. The burial facility is a dual-sleeve {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, oriented southward. It consists of three compartments: a main chamber, a middle chamber (first antechamber), and a front chamber (second antechamber), connected to a corridor. This grand stone chamber measures a total length of 16.5 meters and features massive basalt stones as construction material. Excavations have uncovered iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, and Silla pottery. The mound is dated to the late 6th century, with some burials possibly extending into the 7th century. In {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}, the site was Historic site as part of the National Historic site "Iki Kofun Group." == Archaeological History == * {{nihongo|1841|天保12nd year }}: The ''Kōshi Yawa'' includes illustrations of the tumulus and its stone chamber. * {{nihongo|1861|文久元年}}: The ''Iki Meishō Zushi'' contains sketches and measurements of the mound. * {{nihongo|1936|昭和11th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber conducted by Kyoto University (unpublished). * {{nihongo|1953|昭和28th year }}: Excavations by the Tōa Archaeological Society, with findings reported in {{nihongo|2018|平成30th year }}. * {{nihongo|1961|昭和36th year }}: Designated as a Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site. * {{nihongo|1970|昭和45th year }}: Survey of the stone chamber by Kyushu University, with detailed diagrams published in {{nihongo|1980|昭和55th year }}. * {{nihongo|1987|昭和62nd year }}: Typhoon damage to the right wall of the chamber entrance. * {{nihongo|1989|平成元年}}: Restoration and further investigations by Ashibe Town Board of Education. * {{nihongo|2009|平成21st year }}: Included in the National Historic site Historic site. == burial == The burial facility consists of a three-chamber structure, with details as follows: * Total chamber length: 16.5 m * Main chamber: 3.2 m long, 3 m wide, 3.3 m high * Middle chamber: 4.1 m long, 2.4 m wide, 1.75 m high * Front chamber: 4.1 m long, 2 m wide, 1.9 m high * Corridor: 5.1 m long, 1.8 m wide, 1.8 m high * Entrance: 1.9 m wide, 2.4 m high The chamber features massive basalt stones. A rectangular slab (2.55 m long, 0.75 m wide, 0.13 m thick) within the main chamber suggests the presence of a stone sarcophagus. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|1=Iki City Ikikoku Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11429588}} (Iki City, Ashibe Town) == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Ashibe Town Board of Education, ''Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun Environmental Improvement Project Report,'' 1990. * Iki City Board of Education, ''Kofun Groups on Iki Island: Current Survey,'' 2012. * Kyushu University, ''Archaeological Report of Ikihara Sites,'' 2018. * Togo Yoshida, ''The Great Japanese Gazetteer, Volume 7: Ōu,'' 1902. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oni-no-iwaya Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group - Nagasaki Prefecture Historic site Database] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827855|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[en:Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)]] {{wikidata link|Q130455404}} [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
